text.title: Video Upload State Machine
text.summary: Video processing requires an async polling loop with a max-iteration guard; the PROCESSING state can last 30 seconds to 5 minutes depending on video length.

section.title: State machine

rule.statement: Always add a max-iteration guard to the video processing polling loop — a stuck PROCESSING state loops forever without it.
rule.rationale: Gemini video processing can hang in PROCESSING indefinitely for corrupted or unsupported files; the guard prevents infinite loops.

rule.statement: Handle FAILED state explicitly and raise an error — do not treat it as a retry condition.
rule.rationale: A FAILED state means the file cannot be processed; retrying wastes time and quota.

section.reference: analyze_video() with max-iteration guard and ACTIVE/FAILED handling

example.code: # Extract structured info with timestamps response = model.generate_content([ """Analyze this video and extract: {"topics": [...], "key_timestamps": [{"time": "MM:SS", "event": "..."}], "action_items": [...]} Return only valid JSON.""", video_file ])
example.note: Gemini resolves timestamp references ("what happens at 2:30") natively; no frame extraction needed.

rule.statement: Use GCS URIs (`gs://bucket/file.mp4`) instead of SDK uploads for large or frequently-accessed files — faster, no re-upload if the file already exists in GCS.
rule.rationale: SDK uploads copy the file on every pipeline run; GCS URIs reference existing cloud storage without transfer cost.

example.code:
from vertexai.generative_models import GenerativeModel, Part
video_part = Part.from_uri(uri="gs://your-bucket/video.mp4", mime_type="video/mp4") model = GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro") response = model.generate_content(["Summarize this video", video_part])
